topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the old park bench felt suddenly cold beneath my fingers. I’d been sketching the overgrown ivy for nearly an hour, lost in the quiet hum of the afternoon. Then I saw it—a thick, dark wallet lying half-hidden beneath a cluster of fallen leaves. My heart gave a strange, heavy thump against my ribs. When I picked it up, the weight was substantial, and as I flipped it open, the sight of the bills inside made my breath catch. It was an astonishing amount of cash. A sudden, prickling sensation crawled up my arms. I quickly tucked it into my bag, my movements jerky, and started walking away, the sound of my own footsteps echoing too loudly in the stillness.

[story 2]

It was tucked inside the lining of my coat pocket, a heavy, surprisingly cool mass of paper. I hadn't intended to find it; I was just retrieving my keys. But when I pulled out the wallet, the sheer volume of currency made the air feel thick. My hands began to tremble slightly as I counted the stacks, the numbers swimming before my eyes. I stood there by the doorway for a long moment, staring at the money, a knot tightening in my stomach. Every shadow seemed to shift, and I kept glancing over my shoulder, half-expecting someone to materialize from the periphery. I shoved the wallet deep into my backpack and practically bolted down the street.

[story 3]

I found the abandoned purse near the bus stop. It was heavy, and when I opened it, the crisp, banded notes spilled out, catching the weak sunlight. The sheer quantity of money was staggering. A wave of disorientation washed over me. My mind struggled to process the reality of it—this wasn't just a few hundred dollars; this was a fortune. I sat down on the curb, my gaze darting around the empty street. I kept touching the edge of the wallet, a nervous habit, my jaw working silently as I tried to anchor myself to the present moment, but the silence of the afternoon felt charged, expectant.
